Social Media Image Sizes 2025

Find the correct image dimensions for every platform and content type. Download a blank canvas template at the exact size instantly.

Select Platform

Select Format

Select a format above to see the dimensions

Why do social media image sizes matter?

Each platform has its own display format and will automatically crop, compress or scale images that don't match the expected dimensions. Using the wrong size means your carefully designed graphic might be cut off, blurry or distorted in feeds and notifications. Getting the right dimensions from the start prevents wasted design time and poor engagement caused by bad cropping.

Instagram image sizes 2025

Square post: 1080×1080px (1:1). Portrait post: 1080×1350px (4:5). Landscape post: 1080×566px (1.91:1). Stories and Reels: 1080×1920px (9:16). Profile picture: 320×320px (displayed as a circle). Using the maximum 1080px width ensures your images stay sharp on high-density screens.

YouTube image sizes 2025

Thumbnail: 1280×720px (16:9, minimum 2MB recommended). Channel banner: 2560×1440px (safe zone 1546×423px for display across all devices). Profile picture: 800×800px. Shorts thumbnail: 1080×1920px (9:16). YouTube thumbnails are one of the most important factors for click-through rate — use bold text, high contrast and a face when possible.

LinkedIn image sizes 2025

Profile photo: 400×400px minimum. Cover photo: 1584×396px. Post image: 1200×627px (1.91:1). Company logo: 300×300px. Article cover image: 1920×1080px. LinkedIn posts with images get significantly higher engagement than text-only posts, so using the correct dimensions is especially important for professional reach.

What is the blank canvas download?

A blank white JPG file at exactly the right pixel dimensions for your chosen platform and content type. Open it in Canva, Figma, Photoshop, or any design tool and you'll be starting at the correct size without any setup. It eliminates guesswork and the risk of building a design at the wrong aspect ratio.
